source_title: DOW-UAP-D65, Mission Report, Persian Gulf, July 2020
description_blurb:
This document is a Mission Report (MISREP), a standardized reporting form the U.S. Military uses to record the circumstances surrounding its operations. U.S. military services often use MISREPs to report Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP) to AARO. The GENTEXT, or “general text” section of these reports often contains important qualitative, contextual information, distinguishing it from the more quantitative, or numerical, data found elsewhere in the report.
A U.S. military operator reported encountering three separate UAP on July 16, 2020, at 1830Z, 1920Z, and 2345Z. 
All descriptive and estimative language contained in this report reflects the reporter’s subjective interpretation at the time of the event. Such characterizations should not be interpreted as a conclusive indication of the presence or absence of any intrinsic object features or performance characteristics.
title: DOW-UAP-D65, Mission Report, Persian Gulf, July 2020
summary: A U.S. military operator, flying an aircraft with callsign [1.4a] on an ISR mission supporting NAVCENT, reported three separate observations of Unidentified Aerial Phenomena (UAP) on July 16, 2020, at 1830Z, 1920Z, and 2345Z in the Persian Gulf area. The observations were made using Full Motion Video (FMV). The mission involved conducting scans for identified vessels and UAS activity.
bust_reasoning: The document explicitly labels three distinct observations as 'Unidentified Aerial Phenomenon' by military personnel using advanced FMV sensors. However, the report provides no descriptive details of the UAP's characteristics or anomalous behavior (e.g., kinematics, signature). Crucially, the aircraft's 'Radar Name or Designator' is listed as '-', indicating no radar detection for these events. The mission's objective included scanning for 'UAS activity,' making an unidentified drone a plausible explanation that the document does not rule out.
full_text:
# DOW-UAP-D65, Mission Report, Persian Gulf, July 2020 - Page 1 Declassified by MG Richard A. Harrison USCENTCOM Chief of Staff Declassified on: 16 March 2026 FOUO / Privacy Act applies [redacted] # Misrep 4472514 ## Narrative AT 0443Z, [1.4a] TOOK OFF FROM OKAS. AT 0504Z [1.4a] HANDED OVER FROM THE LRE. FROM 0631Z TO 0050Z [1.4a] FROM 0552Z TO 0012Z [1.4a] SUPPORTED NAVCENT TO OPERATION [ (b)(1)1.4a ] IVO ARABIAN GULF, STRAIT OF HORMUZ AND GULF OF OMAN. SEE ISR LINE 1. AT 0615, [1.4a] RECEIVED A GUARD CALL. SEE GUARD CALL LINE 1. AT 1830 [1.4a] OBSERVED AN UNIDENTIFIED AERIAL PHENOMENON. SEE OBS LINE 1. AT 1920Z, [1.4a] BSERVED AN UNIDENTIFIED AERIAL PHENOMENON. SEE OBS LINE 2. AT 2345Z, [1.4a] BSERVED AN UNIDENTIFIED AERIAL PHENOMENON. SEE OBS LINE 3. AT 0012Z, [1.4a] AS CLEARED TO RTB. AT 0124Z, [1.4a] HANDED BACK TO THE LRE. AT 0200Z [1.4a] LANDED AT OKAS. FMV WAS EXPLOITED BY DGS-1. 20.3 MISSION HOURS, 18.3 IMINT HOURS, 1 IMINT TASKING PROSECUTED, 18.3 [1.4a] HOURS, 1 [1.4a] TASKING PROSECUTED, 2 TOTAL TASKINGS PROSECUTED ## Admin ### CLASSIFICATION * Classification: [redacted] * Associated Caveats: [redacted] * Classification Source (MS or Classification Guide ID): MULTIPLE SOURCES * Declassification Date (YYYYMMDD): 20450301 ### OPERATION * Operation: [ (b)(1)1.4a ] * Domain: AIR * Operations Center: 609th * Major Command (MAJCOM): ACC * Combatant Command (COCOM): USCENTCOM ### MSGID * Report Type: MISREP * Originator (Unit or Squadron): 482ATKS * Submit Date (Auto-generated): [redacted: 3.5c, (b)(6)] USCENTCOM MDR 26-0028 | Approved for Release to AARO - FOUO / PA applies | 03/16/26 000001 # DOW-UAP-D65, Mission Report, Persian Gulf, July 2020 - Page 2 Declassified by MG Richard A. Harrison USCENTCOM Chief of Staff Declassified on: 16 March 2026 FOUO / Privacy Act applies [redacted] - Data Link: - - Gentext: - # Timeline ## On Station - Time On Station DTG: 160504:00ZJUL20 - Callsign: [1.4a] - JTAR NUMBER: - - Killbox (Location): - - Mission Type: AREC - JTAC Callsign: - - Gentext/Additional Details: - - Did not Arrive On Station: ## ISR - Time-on Station DTG: 160552:00ZJUL20 - Time-off Station DTG: 170012:00ZJUL20 - Aircraft Callsign: [1.4a] - Msn Type: AREC - Primary Sensor: ANDAS4 - Sensors Available: AH\GMESH - Tasking Type: PLANNED - Tasking or Request Number (JTAR#, AEM#, PRI#, or TIC#): - - BE Number (if NTISR): - - Tasked Start Point: 39RVN[1.4a] - Activity Description: ISR - FMV or Image File Name (if NTISR): - ## GENTEXT/ISR - Gentext: [redacted] [1.4a] COORDINATED WITH NAVCENT 24 HOURS PRIOR TO TAKE OFF. PRECOORD WAS SATISFACTORY. AT 0552Z, [1.4a] ARRIVED ON STATION ISO NAVCENT TO OPERATION [redacted] [1.4a] TO CONDUCT SCANS FOR IDENTIFIED [1.4a, 1.4g] VESSELS TO ESTABLISH PATTERN OF LIFE, UAS ACTIVITY AND CHARACTERIZE ACTIVITY OUTSIDE OF PORTS. FROM 0552Z TO 0012Z [1.4a] CONDUCTED SCANS IAW THE TARGET DECK IN THE ARABIAN GULF (AG). AT 0615, [1.4a] ECEIVED A GUARD CALL. SEE GUARD CALL LINE 1. AT 0645Z, [1.4a] OBSERVED 1X PROB NASER WAP. AT 1932Z, [1.4a] OBSERVED 1X PROB SAFIR KISH IVO 39RUN[1.4a] FACING EAST. AT 2140Z [1.4a] OBSERVED 1X POSS HOUDONG AND 1X POSS NASER DOCKED AT BUSHEHR [3.5c (b)(6)] [redacted] USCENTCOM MDR 26-0028 Approved for Release to AARO - FOUO / PA applies 03/16/26 000004 # DOW-UAP-D65, Mission Report, Persian Gulf, July 2020 - Page 5 Declassified by MG Richard A. ORDERS GIVEN; STANDARD CALL 1.4a RESPONDED WITH THE STANDARD RESPONSE. NO IMPACT TO MISSION [3.5c (b)(6)] [redacted] USCENTCOM MDR 26-0028 Approved for Release to AARO - FOUO / PA applies 03/16/26 000005 # DOW-UAP-D65, Mission Report, Persian Gulf, July 2020 - Page 6 Declassified by MG Richard A. cover_up_reasoning: The document exhibits substantial concealment. It contains extensive sentence-level black-bar redactions across multiple pages, including the original classification details, associated caveats, key operational names, and all witness contact information. Furthermore, the original declassification date for the full document is set for 2045, indicating that this is a partial release of a document that remains largely classified.
summary_zh: 一名美国军事操作员，驾驶呼号为[1.4a]的飞机执行支援海军中央司令部（NAVCENT）的ISR任务时，于2020年7月16日1830Z、1920Z和2345Z在波斯湾地区报告了三次不明空中现象（UAP）的独立观测。观测通过全动态视频（FMV）进行。该任务涉及对已识别船只和UAS活动进行扫描。
bust_reasoning_zh: 该文件明确将军事人员使用先进FMV传感器进行的三次独立观测标记为“不明空中现象”。然而，报告未提供UAP特征或异常行为（例如，运动学、信号特征）的描述性细节。至关重要的是，该飞机的“雷达名称或代号”被列为“-”，表明这些事件未被雷达探测到。该任务的目标包括扫描“UAS活动”，这使得不明无人机成为文件未排除的合理解释。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件存在大量隐瞒。文件多页包含大范围的句子级黑条遮盖，包括原始密级详情、相关警示、关键行动名称以及所有目击者联系信息。此外，该文件的完整解密日期设定为2045年，表明这仅是大部分仍属机密文件的一次部分公开。
claims: A U.S. military operator observed three separate UAPs
Observations occurred at 1830Z, 1920Z, and 2345Z on July 16, 2020
Observations were made via Full Motion Video (FMV)
claims_zh: 一名美国军事操作员观测到三次独立的UAP
观测通过全动态视频（FMV）进行
bust_explanations: Military drone (e.g., foreign surveillance drone, unidentified friendly drone) given the mission context of scanning for 'UAS activity' and the lack of descriptive details.
Consumer drone (e.g., civilian drone operating in restricted airspace) due to the location near ports and the absence of specific anomalous behavior.
Distant aircraft or balloon, misidentified due to the lack of visual detail in the report and the sole reliance on FMV for observation, with no radar corroboration.
bust_explanations_zh: 军用无人机（例如，外国侦察无人机、不明友方无人机），考虑到任务背景是扫描“UAS活动”且缺乏描述性细节。
消费级无人机（例如，在禁飞区运行的民用无人机），鉴于地点靠近港口且缺乏具体的异常行为。
远距离飞机或气球，因报告中缺乏视觉细节且仅依赖FMV进行观测，无雷达佐证而误判。
cover_up_indicators: Original classification details and associated caveats are redacted
Extensive sentence-level black-bar redactions across multiple pages
Key operational names and details (e.g., operation name, vessel types) are withheld
All witness names and contact information (POC, QC, Approver) are redacted
The original declassification date for the full document is 2045, indicating a partial release
cover_up_indicators_zh: 原始密级详情及相关警示被遮盖
多页包含大范围的句子级黑条遮盖
关键行动名称和细节（例如，行动名称、船只类型）被隐瞒
所有目击者姓名和联系信息（POC、QC、批准人）被遮盖
该文件的完整解密日期为2045年，表明这是一次部分公开
